annotations.label: Cauchy's integral formula
annotations.note: Gives ∮ (z-w)⁻¹•f z = 2πi•f w under continuity on the closed disc and differentiability on the interior, which is more general than the article's holomorphic-on-an-open-set hypothesis.

annotations.label: Cauchy's differentiation formula (analyticity)

anchor.snippet: it follows that holomorphic functions are analytic

annotations.note: States that a function differentiable on an open set in ℂ is analytic (admits a power series) on that set.

anchors.snippet: it follows that holomorphic functions are analytic

annotations.label: Generalization to rectifiable curves

anchor.snippet: can be replaced by any closed rectifiable curve in

annotations.note: Mathlib's Cauchy formula is stated only for circles via circleIntegral, with no version for arbitrary closed rectifiable curves and winding numbers.

annotations.label: Not every boundary function arises

anchor.snippet: not every continuous function on the boundary can be used to produce a function inside the boundary

annotations.note: This remark about boundary functions not all extending holomorphically has no corresponding statement in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Evaluating a contour integral
annotations.note: The general machinery (∮ (z-w)⁻¹ = 2πi and the Cauchy formula) exists, but this specific worked numerical example on the circle of radius 2 is not present.

annotations.label: Holomorphic implies infinitely differentiable

anchor.snippet: a function that is holomorphic in an open set is in fact infinitely differentiable there

annotations.note: Shows a function differentiable on an open set in ℂ is ContDiffOn of any order n (including ∞), i.e. infinitely differentiable there.

anchors.snippet: a function that is holomorphic in an open set is in fact infinitely differentiable there

annotations.label: Uniform limit of holomorphic functions

anchor.snippet: the uniform limit of holomorphic functions is holomorphic

annotations.note: Establishes that a locally uniform limit of holomorphic functions on an open set is holomorphic, generalizing the uniform-limit statement.

annotations.label: Cauchy's estimate
annotations.note: Bounds the n-th derivative at the center by n!·C/Rⁿ given a bound C on the boundary circle, exactly Cauchy's estimate.

annotations.label: Liouville's theorem

anchor.snippet: every bounded entire function must be constant

annotations.note: Proves a complex-differentiable function with bounded range is constant, stated for Banach-space-valued maps and hence more general than entire ℂ→ℂ functions.

annotations.label: Gauss's mean-value theorem
annotations.note: States that the circle average of a holomorphic function equals its value at the center, i.e. Gauss's mean-value property.

annotations.note: Mathlib has the boundary-integral-to-area-integral relation involving ∂f/∂z̄ on rectangles, but not the disc-based Cauchy–Pompeiu formula with the 1/(z-w) kernel.

annotations.label: Cauchy kernel is a fundamental solution

anchor.snippet: the Cauchy kernel is a fundamental solution of the Cauchy–Riemann equations

annotations.note: Mathlib lacks a distributional/fundamental-solution statement for the Cauchy kernel of the ∂/∂z̄ operator.

anchors.snippet: the Cauchy kernel is a fundamental solution of the Cauchy–Riemann equations

annotations.label: Distributional derivative of characteristic function

anchor.snippet: the formula for the distributional derivative of the characteristic function

annotations.note: No formula for the distributional derivative of a domain's characteristic function is present in Mathlib.

anchors.snippet: the formula for the distributional derivative of the characteristic function

annotations.label: Cauchy formula on polydiscs

anchor.snippet: the Cauchy integral formula can be generalized to polydiscs

annotations.note: Polydiscs appear only in a comment in Mathlib; no several-variable Cauchy integral formula is formalized.

anchors.snippet: the Cauchy integral formula can be generalized to polydiscs

annotations.label: Green's function of the derivative operator

anchor.snippet: This particular derivative operator has a Green's function

annotations.note: No Green's function for a Clifford-algebra Dirac-type derivative operator is formalized in Mathlib.

anchors.snippet: This particular derivative operator has a Green's function

annotations.label: Monogenic function
annotations.note: Mathlib has no definition of monogenic functions in Clifford/geometric algebra.

annotations.label: Cauchy formula in real algebras

anchor.snippet: the value of an analytic (monogenic) function at a point can be found by an integral over the surface surrounding the point

annotations.note: The Clifford-analysis surface-integral Cauchy formula for monogenic functions is not present in Mathlib.
